documentation.file_docstring: Synology Chat HTTP client. Sends messages TO Synology Chat via the incoming webhook URL.
inline_comments: --- Chat user_id resolution ---
Synology Chat uses two different user_id spaces:
- Outgoing webhook user_id: per-integration sequential ID (e.g. 1)
- Chat API user_id: global internal ID (e.g. 4)
The chatbot API (method=chatbot) requires the Chat API user_id in the
user_ids array. We resolve via the user_list API and cache the result.
Cache user lists per bot endpoint to avoid cross-account bleed.

Send a text message to Synology Chat via the incoming webhook. @param incomingUrl - Synology Chat incoming webhook URL @param text - Message text to send @param userId - Optional user ID to mention with @ @returns true if sent successfully
Synology Chat API requires user_ids (numeric) to specify the recipient
The @mention is optional but user_ids is mandatory
Internal rate limit: min 500ms between sends
Retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 300ms base)
will retry
Send a file URL to Synology Chat.

Fetch the list of Chat users visible to this bot via the user_list API. Results are cached for CACHE_TTL_MS to avoid excessive API calls. The user_list endpoint uses the same base URL as the chatbot API but with method=user_list instead of method=chatbot.

Resolve a webhook username to the correct Chat API user_id. Synology Chat outgoing webhooks send a user_id that may NOT match the Chat-internal user_id needed by the chatbot API (method=chatbot). The webhook's "username" field corresponds to the Chat user's "nickname". @param incomingUrl - Bot incoming webhook URL (used to derive user_list URL) @param webhookUsername - The username from the outgoing webhook payload @param allowInsecureSsl - Skip TLS verification @returns The correct Chat user_id, or undefined if not found
Match by nickname first (webhook "username" field = Chat "nickname")
Then by username
Synology NAS may use self-signed certs on local network.
Set allowInsecureSsl: true in channel config to skip verification.
